audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for earthly tent/heavenly dwelling, walking by trust, judgment seat, love of Messiah, new creation, reconciliation, ambassadors.

verses.restored: For we know that if our earthly house of the tent is taken down, we have a building from the Divine, a house not made with hands, age-long in the heavens.
For in this we groan, longing to be clothed over with our dwelling from heaven,
if indeed, having been clothed, we will not be found naked.
For while we are in the tent, we groan, being burdened, not because we wish to be unclothed, but clothed over, so that the mortal may be swallowed by life.
The One who worked us into this very thing is the Divine, who gave us the pledge/down payment of the Spirit.
Therefore, always being courageous and knowing that while at home in the body we are away from home from the Lord,
for we walk through trust, not through sight.
Yet we are courageous and think it beautiful/good rather to be away from home from the body and at home with the Lord.
Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or away, to be pleasing to him.
For we all must be manifested before the judgment seat of Messiah, so that each may receive the things through the body according to what one practiced, whether good or harmful.
Knowing therefore the reverence/fear of the Lord, we persuade humans, and we have been made manifest to the Divine; and I hope also in your consciences we have been made manifest.
We are not again recommending ourselves to you, but giving you opportunity for boasting on our behalf, so that you may have an answer toward those boasting in face and not in heart.
For if we were out of our mind, it was for the Divine; if sober-minded, it is for you.
For the love of Messiah holds us together, having judged this: that one died for all; therefore all died.
And he died for all, so that those living may no longer live for themselves, but for the One who died for them and was raised.
So then, from now on we know no one according to flesh. Even if we have known Messiah according to flesh, yet now we no longer know him so.
So if anyone is in Messiah: new creation. The old things passed away; look, new things have come to be.
But all things are from the Divine, who reconciled us to himself through Messiah and gave us the service/ministry of reconciliation,
namely, that the Divine was in Messiah reconciling the world to himself, not counting their trespasses to them, and placing in us the word of reconciliation.
Therefore we are ambassadors on behalf of Messiah, as the Divine appealing through us. We ask on behalf of Messiah: be reconciled to the Divine.
The One who knew no sin/misalignment he made sin/misalignment for us, so that in him we might become righteousness/justice of the Divine.

translation_notes: Tent/house/clothing imagery is embodied resurrection hope, not contempt for the body. The mortal is swallowed by life, not erased.
Reconciliation is central: God in Messiah reconciles the world and entrusts the community with the word and service of reconciliation.
